routes.name: MAIN ROUTE — Days 0-6 (Sydney → Coober Pedy)
routes.desc: Brighton-le-Sands → Berrima → Goulburn → Jugiong → Wagga → Hay → Balranald → Mungo NP → Menindee → Broken Hill → Mundi Mundi → Cockburn → Olary → Peterborough → Port Augusta → Woomera → Coober Pedy

routes.name: RETURN Route A — Via Adelaide + Barossa + Murray (Days 7-9)
routes.desc: Coober Pedy → Port Augusta → Adelaide → Barossa Valley → Clare → Morgan → Renmark → Mildura → Balranald → Hay → Wagga → Sydney. Best for food, wine, Murray River.

routes.name: RETURN Route B — Via Flinders Ranges + Clare (Days 7-9)
routes.desc: Coober Pedy → Port Augusta → Quorn → Hawker → Wilpena Pound → Brachina Gorge → Orroroo → Peterborough → Clare Valley → Morgan → Renmark → Mildura → Sydney. Best scenery.

routes.name: RETURN Route C — Via Broken Hill + Barrier Hwy + Bathurst (Days 7-9)
routes.desc: Coober Pedy → Port Augusta → Peterborough → Broken Hill → Wilcannia → Cobar → Nyngan → Dubbo → Orange → Bathurst (Mount Panorama) → Lithgow → Sydney. Best for outback NSW and motorsport.

markers.name: Day 0 DEPART — Brighton-le-Sands
markers.desc: Departure point. Leave ~4pm Tuesday 18 August. M5 southwest onto Hume Hwy.

markers.name: Option 1 — Goulburn (Night of 18 Aug)
markers.desc: ~200 km, ~3 hrs with traffic. Arrive ~7pm. Stay: Discovery Holiday Park Goulburn (powered caravan sites). Pub: Rocky Brewing Co (Auburn St). Easiest early night.

markers.name: Option 2 — Jugiong / Sir George (Night of 18 Aug)
markers.desc: ~285 km, ~3.5 hrs. Arrive ~7:30pm. Stay: Jugiong Caravan Park (riverside, basic). Pub/Dinner: Sir George — one of the great Hume Hwy pubs. Best experience of the three options.

markers.name: Option 3 — Wagga Wagga (Night of 18 Aug)
markers.desc: ~440 km, ~5 hrs. Arrive ~9-9:30pm (late). Stay: Wagga Wagga Tourist Park. Push option — latest arrival but shortest Day 1 drive. Eat on the road at Jugiong or a servo.

markers.name: Day 1 STAY — Mungo NP Main Camp (Night of 19 Aug)
markers.desc: Mungo National Park Main Campground. Powered caravan sites. Book via nationalparks.nsw.gov.au. $8.50/person/day entry. PRIME STARGAZING NIGHT — moon sets ~9:30pm, pitch dark 10pm onwards. One of the best dark-sky sites in Australia.

markers.name: Day 2-4 STAY — Mundi Mundi Festival Site (20-22 Aug)
markers.desc: Belmont Station, Mundi Mundi Plains — 35 km north of Broken Hill. Festival camp. BYO food and drinks permitted. Facilities on site. Nights drop to 0-3C — bring warm gear.

markers.name: Day 5 STAY — Woomera Travellers Village (Night of 23 Aug)
markers.desc: Caravan-friendly powered sites. Cold War rocket testing town — Missile Park and Heritage Centre are free and extraordinary. Eldo Hotel for dinner.

markers.name: Day 6 STAY — Desert Cave Hotel, Coober Pedy (Night of 24 Aug)
markers.desc: Underground rooms carved from sandstone. The most iconic accommodation in outback Australia. Book well ahead: desertcave.com.au. Tom & Mary's Taverna underground for dinner.

markers.name: PUB — Surveyor General Inn, Berrima
markers.desc: Day 0 dinner stop. Australia's oldest continuously licensed hotel, est. 1835. Stone building, open fire, excellent food. 1 km off the Hume Hwy. Turn off at Mittagong/Bowral exit.

markers.name: PUB — Bidgee Hotel, Hay
markers.desc: Day 1 lunch stop. Classic outback country pub. Good pub grub. Last proper town before Mungo NP — also fuel and supplies here.

markers.name: PUB — Crown Hotel, Balranald
markers.desc: Day 1 stretch stop. Est. 1882. Cold beer, last stop before the unsealed road into Mungo NP. Last reliable fuel.

markers.name: PUB — Maiden Hotel, Menindee
markers.desc: Day 2 lunch stop. Burke and Wills rested here in 1860 — three days before their disastrous push north. Cold beer, Darling River views. Non-negotiable lunch stop on the Broken Hill run.

markers.name: PUB — Palace Hotel, Broken Hill
markers.desc: Day 2 afternoon stop. Priscilla Queen of the Desert filming location. Extraordinary murals inside. Non-negotiable schooner.

markers.name: PUB — Silverton Hotel, Silverton
markers.desc: Days 3-4. Population 37. Real bullet holes in the walls. Mad Max 2 filming location. A donkey in the yard. 25 km from Broken Hill — the best outback pub in NSW. Non-negotiable.

markers.name: PUB — Olary Hotel, Olary SA
markers.desc: Day 5. Heritage-listed. Population ~20. One of the most isolated pubs in South Australia. Cold beer in the void. A 15-minute stretch stop worth doing.

markers.name: PUB — Transcontinental Hotel, Peterborough SA
markers.desc: Day 5 lunch stop. Heritage railway junction town where three gauges met. Great schnitty, cold beer. Also check out the Steam Town museum (45 min, optional).

markers.name: PUB — Eldo Hotel, Woomera
markers.desc: Day 5 dinner. The pub in Woomera. Cold beer, outback government-town vibe, feeds the workers.

markers.name: PUB — Tom & Mary's Taverna, Coober Pedy
markers.desc: Day 6 dinner. Underground restaurant and bar. Greek-Italian. Genuinely excellent food for the middle of nowhere. One of the best meals on the whole trip.

markers.name: PUB — Opal Inn Hotel, Coober Pedy
markers.desc: Day 6. Main pub in Coober Pedy. Lively, cold beer, session atmosphere.

markers.name: PUB [Route A] — The Exeter Hotel, Adelaide
markers.desc: Route A, Day 7. The soul of Adelaide pub culture on Rundle Street. Cold beer, great atmosphere, starting point for Adelaide pub crawl.

markers.name: PUB [Route A] — Renmark Hotel, Renmark
markers.desc: Route A, Day 8. 1897 riverfront heritage pub on the Murray. One of the great country pubs in SA.

markers.name: PUB/REST [Route A] — Stefano's Grand Hotel, Mildura
markers.desc: Route A, Day 8. Stefano de Pieri's legendary Italian restaurant at the Grand Hotel. Book ahead — one of the best restaurants in regional Australia.

markers.name: REST [Route B] — Old Ghan Restaurant, Hawker
markers.desc: Route B, Day 7. Former station master's residence, good outback meals. Last stop before Wilpena Pound.

markers.name: REST [Route B] — Skillogalee Winery, Clare Valley
markers.desc: Route B, Day 8. Best lunch restaurant in the Clare Valley. Book ahead.

markers.name: PUB [Route B] — Renmark Hotel, Renmark
markers.desc: Route B, Day 8. Same as Route A — 1897 riverfront heritage pub on the Murray.

markers.name: PUB [Route C] — Mario's Palace Hotel, Broken Hill
markers.desc: Route C, Day 7. The actual Priscilla Queen of the Desert hotel. Stay here for one night — mural-covered rooms, great pub downstairs.

markers.name: PUB [Route C] — Imperial Hotel, Wilcannia
markers.desc: Route C, Day 8. Cold beer and Darling River history. Historic sandstone town.

markers.name: PUB [Route C] — Royal Hotel, Cobar
markers.desc: Route C, Day 8. Est. 1898. One of the most spectacular two-storey cast-iron lace verandahs in Australia — a 50-metre facade. Lunch here. Non-negotiable.

markers.name: FUEL — Goulburn
markers.desc: Day 0/1 — Multiple servos. Fill up before continuing southwest.

markers.name: FUEL — Wagga Wagga
markers.desc: Day 1 — Fill up here before continuing to Narrandera/Hay.

markers.name: FUEL — Hay
markers.desc: Day 1 — IMPORTANT fuel stop. Fill up here. Last reliable fuel before Mungo NP (via Balranald). Also last town with food options.

markers.name: FUEL — Balranald
markers.desc: Day 1 — Last fuel before the unsealed road into Mungo NP. Also last reliable phone coverage. Download offline maps here.

markers.name: FUEL — Menindee
markers.desc: Day 2 — Fuel available. Worth topping up before the Broken Hill run.

markers.name: FUEL — Broken Hill
markers.desc: Day 2 — Fill ALL vehicles and jerry cans here. Last reliable fuel before Mundi Mundi. Multiple servos (Caltex, BP, United).

markers.name: FUEL — Port Augusta
markers.desc: Day 5 — Major fuel and supplies stop. Crossroads of Australia. Top up before Woomera.

markers.name: FUEL — Glendambo
markers.desc: Day 6 — CRITICAL fuel stop on Stuart Hwy between Port Augusta and Coober Pedy. There is NOTHING for 250 km either side. Do not skip.

markers.name: FUEL — Woomera
markers.desc: Day 6 — Top up before Stuart Hwy north to Coober Pedy.

markers.name: FUEL [Route A] — Balranald (return)
markers.desc: Route A, Day 9 — Fuel stop on the return run Mildura to Hay.

markers.name: FUEL [Route C] — Nyngan
markers.desc: Route C, Day 8 — Fuel and stretch. The Bogan River runs through here.

markers.name: FUEL [Route C] — Lithgow
markers.desc: Route C, Day 9 — Final fuel stop before Sydney on the Bathurst run.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Walls of China (The Lunette), Mungo NP
markers.desc: Day 1. The iconic crescent-shaped eroded landform at Mungo NP. UNESCO World Heritage. 50,000-year-old human remains found here — oldest outside Africa. Afternoon light is extraordinary. Walk the viewing platform.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Red Top Lookout, Mungo NP
markers.desc: Day 1. Best panoramic view over the ancient dry lake bed. Go at sunset.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Mungo NP Visitor Centre
markers.desc: Day 1. Check ranger walk availability on arrival. Maps, park info, book astronomy program if available.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Line of Lode Miners Memorial, Broken Hill
markers.desc: Day 2. Take the lift to the top of the old mine shaft. Panoramic views over Broken Hill and the slag heaps. Most moving tribute to Australian mining history.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Pro Hart Gallery, Broken Hill
markers.desc: Day 2. Broken Hill's most famous artist, internationally renowned. Worth 20 minutes even if you're not an art person.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — The Big Picture Gallery (Silver City Mint)
markers.desc: Day 2. The world's longest landscape painting — 100 metres. Genuinely impressive.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Mundi Mundi Lookout
markers.desc: Days 3-4. 360-degree views over the Mundi Mundi Plains. Spectacular at sunset. One of the great outback panoramas in Australia.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Silverton (Mad Max town)
markers.desc: Days 3-4. Population 37. Mad Max 2, Razorback, and a dozen other films shot here. The Silverton Hotel has actual bullet holes in the walls. Get there at lunch before the bus tours.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Woomera Missile Park
markers.desc: Day 5. Open-air display of Cold War rockets, guided missiles, and aircraft. Free. Genuinely impressive — Australia's secret role in Cold War weapons testing.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Woomera Heritage Centre
markers.desc: Day 5. The history of the Blue Streak missile program, the space race, and Australia's classified Cold War history. More gripping than it sounds.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Old Timers Mine, Coober Pedy
markers.desc: Day 6. Best mine tour in Coober Pedy. Original 1916 dugout home, hands-on opal mining experience.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — Underground St Peter & Paul Church, Coober Pedy
markers.desc: Day 6. A church carved into rock. Stained glass, altar, extraordinary silence. One of the most remarkable places in outback Australia.

markers.name: ATTRACTION — The Breakaways, Coober Pedy
markers.desc: Day 6. 25 km north of town. Mesa/escarpment landscape that looks like Mars. Drive out for sunset (~5:30pm in August). One of the most photographed landscapes in outback SA.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route A] — Adelaide Central Market
markers.desc: Route A, Day 7. Best food market in Australia (open until 5:30pm, closed Sunday). Deli, produce, coffee. Go on arrival in Adelaide.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route A] — Seppeltsfield, Barossa Valley
markers.desc: Route A, Day 8. Historic Barossa winery. The 100-year-old Centennial Collection para vintage tawny is a must-taste. Great cellar door.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route B] — Wilpena Pound, Flinders Ranges
markers.desc: Route B, Day 7. Natural amphitheatre — a ring of quartzite mountains enclosing a vast flat basin. One of the great natural landmarks of South Australia. Walking tracks from the resort.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route B] — Brachina Gorge, Flinders Ranges
markers.desc: Route B, Day 8. Drive through at dawn. 600 million-year-old rock walls — UNESCO geoheritage site. The colours at sunrise are extraordinary.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route C] — Great Cobar Heritage Centre
markers.desc: Route C, Day 8. The history of the copper mining boom. Good 45-min stop before or after lunch at the Royal Hotel.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route C] — Mount Panorama Circuit, Bathurst
markers.desc: Route C, Day 9. MANDATORY STOP (especially for Rob). The 6.2 km Bathurst 1000 race circuit is a public road — drive a lap (speed limits apply). National Motor Racing Museum at the base ($20pp, 45 min). One of the great motorsport venues in the world.

markers.name: ATTRACTION [Route C] — Taronga Western Plains Zoo, Dubbo
markers.desc: Route C, Day 8. Open-range zoo with lions, cheetahs, giraffes, rhinos. The only savanna zoo setting in Australia. Evening entry possible (~4:30pm).

markers.name: STARGAZE — Mungo NP (Prime Night: 19 Aug)
markers.desc: BEST STARGAZING NIGHT OF THE TRIP. Moon (waxing crescent) sets ~9:30pm. Completely dark sky from 10pm. Milky Way core, Magellanic Clouds, Coal Sack Nebula, zodiacal light before dawn. One of Australia's premier dark-sky sites — 360-degree horizon, zero light pollution.

markers.name: STARGAZE — Mundi Mundi Plains (20-22 Aug)
markers.desc: Festival nights. Moon rises later each night but sets mid-evening. Late-night music under the Milky Way is the signature Mundi Mundi experience.

markers.name: STARGAZE — Coober Pedy (24 Aug)
markers.desc: Moon approaching full by 24 Aug (~80% illuminated). Still worth looking up — zero light pollution. From The Breakaways in particular the sky is exceptional.
